notes: Mistral Vibe's autonomous loop is the /loop scheduled self-prompt (vibe/core/loop.py): /loop 30s <prompt> re-fires the prompt on a >=30s timer, each fire a discrete turn submitted from idle, until /loop cancel all. Autonomous multi-turn self-prompting (no user input between turns) but NOT goal-convergence — hence agent=partial and, like the claudecode sibling, the arc is per-iteration ready→working→ready rather than one continuous working span. /loop fires recurring turns on ONE session: two scheduled fires, each a clean turn on the SAME real session_<id>, then /loop cancel all returns the session to ready. mistral-vibe is a PRESESSION adapter — a transient proc-<PID> row is born ready BEFORE the real session_<id> transcript dir reconciles under a DIFFERENT session_id; modeled as presession_birth → session_birth(new_session) → pid_bind, every later phase same_session_as session_birth. BACKFLOW re-spec against the LIVE recording (validation drift; recording committed as-is): vibe's /loop enforces a hard 30s-MINIMUM scheduling interval, so the FIRST loop fire — which is when the real session_<id> transcript dir first materializes — lands ~42s after launch, PAST the original 30000ms session_birth window and cascading every downstream anchor. FIX: widened session_birth max_delay_ms 30000→60000 to accommodate the >=30s interval; the observable is UNCHANGED (two clean autonomous working→ready fires on one session, no waiting) — only the timing tolerance moved. Offline replay cannot reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar (model + tokens are sidecar-only), so both are UNVERIFIABLE offline — observations assert agent only (cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false), matching kiro-cli.

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the transcript dir exists)

Real session_<id> reconciles (different session_id) when the transcript directory appears at the FIRST /loop fire, born ready. Window widened to 60000ms: vibe's /loop enforces a >=30s minimum interval so the first fire lands ~42s after launch
vibe PID bound to the real session (matched by cwd)
The first /loop fire injects the scheduled prompt with no user input — ready → working for iteration 1 on the real session
Iteration 1 completes back to ready; vibe holds ready for the >=30s scheduling interval before the next fire
The second scheduled fire drives iteration 2 ready → working with no user input between turns — the autonomous loop is active (>= 2 turns)
/loop cancel all stops the scheduler; the session settles in ready and never entered waiting (no permission gates)
